articles.author: toi lifestyle desk
articles.title: Sweet treat in space? Scientists find ‘raspberry sugar’ deep inside the Milky Way — and it might hold clues to how life began
articles.description: Astronomers have made an exciting find with the detection of erythrulose, a natural sugar molecule, floating near the Milky Way's center. This sugar shares similarities with that found in raspberries, suggesting it could act as a crucial building block for life forms. Researchers posit that comets may have transported these vital sugars to early Earth, hinting that the components of life are prevalent throughout the cosmos.

articles.author: pritha chakraborty
articles.title: What happened today (July 14) in history? Key events to look back on
articles.description: Why is July 14 historic? From Bastille Day to Pluto's first close-up images, discover the events that changed history and science.

articles.author: tanuj lakhina
articles.title: Team India look to shake off T20I hangover as road to ODI World Cup begins
articles.description: After a disastrous T20I tour, India begin their ODI World Cup build-up against England at Edgbaston. With injuries forcing changes, Shubman Gill's side will look to reset, test combinations and lean on the experience of Virat Kohli, Rohit Sharma and Jasprit Bumrah as preparations for the 2027 World Cup gather pace.

articles.title: Anthropic's Claude Mythos Has Canada On Alert, Banks Told To Brace For New AI Threat
articles.description: Canada warns banks about Anthropic's Claude Mythos, saying the AI model could help attackers identify vulnerabilities faster and increase cyber risks.

articles.author: shivam chopra
articles.title: iOS 27 Public Beta Opens to All iPhone Users With Liquid Glass and Faster Siri
articles.description: Apple on Monday opened the iOS 27 public beta to any iPhone user with an Apple ID, making the Liquid Glass redesign and rebuilt Siri available outside the developer program for the first time. App launches are up to 30 percent faster, but some advanced Siri features remain absent.

articles.title: US nuclear fuel firm Clean Core counters BARC’s safety concerns over thorium fuel
articles.description: The BARC paper had argued that deploying ANEEL in India’s existing PHWR fleet will require reactor re-designs and could increase safety risks under accident conditions
